description_long: This sculpture depicts a delicately crafted human hand, rendered in a white, marble-like material. The hand is positioned upright with fingers gracefully extended and slightly curled, as if in a gesture of offering or blessing. The base of the sculpture is rounded and features a rough, textured patch that contrasts with the otherwise smooth surface, suggesting either intentional artistic distressing or natural wear. The fingers are slender and detailed, with visible knuckles and subtle anatomical features. There is a small hole visible on the back of the hand, possibly from damage or as part of the sculpting process. The overall form is elegant and expressive, capturing a sense of movement and poise.
description: A white marble-like sculpture of an upright human hand with gracefully extended fingers and a textured base, exuding elegance and detail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the palm, extended fingers, and the rough, textured patch at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the thumb, the curvature of the fingers, and a visible hole on the back of the hand.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the hand, smooth surface, and the subtle anatomical contours.
description_view_3: Opposite side view focusing on the outer edge of the hand and the textured base.
attribute_location_context: No specific setting or environment is visible; the sculpture appears isolated against a plain background.
